Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 209. (no title) (CT 53 090)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL be-lí-ia]

(1) [To the king, my lord]: your servant [PN]. Good health to [the king, my lord]! May Adad and Buru bless [the king], my lord!

o 22

⸢ARAD*-ka*⸣ [mx x x x x]

o 33

lu-u DI-mu a-[na LUGAL be-lí-ía]

o 44

dIM dAMAR ⸢a⸣-na [LUGAL]

o 55

be-lí-ía lik-⸢ru-bu⸣

o 66

un-qu ⸢ša⸣ LUGAL ⸢be⸣-[lí-ía]

(6) On the 10th Dadâ [bro]ught me a sealed order of the king [my lo]rd concerning the news of [...... Ad]ad-ir[i ......

o 77

ina UGU ṭè-[me] ša m⸢x⸣+[x x x]

o 88

mda-da-a UD 10-[KÁM]

o 99

[it-tu]-⸢bi*⸣-la ⸢bi⸣-[x x]

[x x x md]⸢IM⸣—i-⸢ri⸣-[ba x x]

rr uninscribed

(Rest destroyed or too broken for translation)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P313505/.
